_comment: Cheffy dialogue tree. Data, not code -- copy and options change here without touching components. See CHEFFY-SYSTEM.md > Dialogue system.
_start: Id of the node Cheffy opens on.
_node_shape: Each entry in 'nodes' is { text, expression?, input?, options:[{label, goto? , action?}] }. 'goto' moves to another node id; 'action' runs a handler. expression may be ANY pose or alias from src/data/cheffy-poses.json (neutral, hidden, excited, happy, thinking, sleepy, wink, scowl, surprised, determined, smile, ...); unknown names fall back to neutral. input:true shows a free-text field. A 'goto' starting with '#' is a DYNAMIC ref (built at runtime by cheffyDialogue.ts's resolveDynamicNode, not looked up here) -- e.g. '#archive-browse:0', '#archive-week:<slug>', '#archive-results:<query>'. Static and dynamic gotos can point at each other freely.
_actions: Valid option 'action' values the runtime must implement: generate-ics | generate-ics-meal | open-google-calendar | trigger-permission | notification-status | clear-reminders | navigate-to-archive | navigate-to-lunch | export-checklist | import-checklist | close.

root.text: Hi, I'm Cheffy! What can I do for you?

options.label: Add cook times to my calendar

archive.text: Want to browse old weeks, or hunt for a dish?

calendar.text: I can add this week's cook times to your calendar -- with a heads-up alarm built in.

notifications.text: I can ping you at cook times and lunch times, plus a nudge when the new week's menu lands. Reminders only know THIS week -- when I nudge you, pop back in and re-sync.

checklist.text: I can export or import this week's grocery checklist.

search.text: Name a dish or a week - I'll dig through my cookbook.

lunch.text: Mom's lunch plan lives on its own page -- one easy lunch a day, already folded into the grocery list.

options.label: Take me to the lunch page
